name: Diamond Mountain Mining
short_description: Hunt for famous Herkimer Diamonds and clear quartz crystals in this Mohawk Valley location. A premier destination for collecting double-terminated crystals in New York's mineral-rich region.
description:
Diamond Mountain Mining is located in Herkimer County, the historic heart of Herkimer Diamond country in central New York. This area is renowned for producing some of the finest double-terminated quartz crystals found anywhere in North America. The site sits at a gentle elevation of 635 feet in the Mohawk Valley, an area with a 200-year tradition of crystal hunting.

The geology here reflects the ancient Ordovician Period when shallow seas covered this region. The local dolomite and dolostone bedrock contains vugs and cavities where quartz crystals formed as silicon-rich groundwater percolated through the rock. Herkimer Diamonds are not actual diamonds but rather clear, naturally faceted quartz crystals with distinctive pointed terminations on both ends. The crystals formed within pockets of the Mohawk Dolomite formation, making Herkimer County the only place in the world where this specific crystal habit occurs regularly.

Visitors to this location can search loose rock and small outcrops for crystals ranging from tiny points to larger specimens. The gentle slope and low elevation make this an accessible site for families and beginning rockhounds. Many collectors report finding small to medium crystals with some effort and patience, particularly after seasonal weathering exposes new material.

The area benefits from being in a well-developed region with good road access and nearby services. The Mohawk Valley has supported crystal collecting for generations, and this location continues that tradition. Whether you are a seasoned collector seeking pristine specimens or a beginner looking to find your first Herkimer Diamond, this site offers genuine New York mineral collecting.
history: Herkimer County's quartz crystals formed during the Ordovician Period, roughly 450 million years ago, when silicon-rich fluids filled cavities in the Mohawk Dolomite bedrock. The area gained fame in the 1800s as enthusiasts discovered the distinctive double-terminated crystals, leading to organized collecting that continues today.
tips: Search in loose talus and weathered dolostone outcrops where crystals naturally weather free from the host rock. Use a small rock hammer or chisel to gently tap around cavity openings, and look for the characteristic transparency and pointed terminations. Early morning or after rain when rocks are wet is often best, as the wet surface makes small crystals more visible. Focus on areas where previous collectors have worked, as these zones often contain accessible material.
terrain_notes: Gentle slope at 635 feet elevation in the Mohawk Valley. Terrain is accessible with mixed grassy and rocky ground. Walking distance from parking to collecting areas is typically short, usually under 0.5 miles. Footing is generally stable but can be uneven with loose rock and small outcrops. No extreme elevation or steep sections.
best_season: May - October. Late spring through early fall offers the most comfortable collecting conditions. Summer can be warm but not extreme. Early fall is excellent with mild temperatures. Winter snow and freeze-thaw cycles expose new material but access becomes difficult.
written_directions: From the Town of Little Falls, head north on NY-5 toward Mohawk. Diamond Mountain Mining is in the Herkimer Diamond region northeast of Little Falls. Use GPS coordinates 43.015, -74.874 to navigate to the site. Local roads in the area are rural but maintained. Approach from NY-5 and follow local roads toward the collecting area. Confirm current access status and directions with local rockhounding clubs or landowner before visiting.
road_conditions: Rural maintained roads, passenger vehicle access
parking_notes: Limited informal parking near collecting area. Space for a few vehicles. No formal parking facility.
collection_rules: Private land - permission from owner required before collecting. No commercial use. Respect property boundaries. Pack out all trash. No motorized equipment or excavation without permission.
rules:
Permission from owner required
No commercial collection
No motorized equipment without permission
Pack out all trash
Respect property boundaries
nearest_services: Town of Little Falls approximately 3 miles away with gas, food, and water services
accessibility_notes: Relatively flat and gentle sloped terrain accessible to most hikers and families. No wheelchair access to collecting areas. Sturdy footwear recommended for uneven rocky ground. Short walking distances make it suitable for children and those with limited mobility who can navigate loose rock.

faq.question: Where can I find Herkimer Diamonds near Little Falls, New York?
faq.answer: Diamond Mountain Mining in Herkimer County is one of the premier locations for Herkimer Diamonds. These naturally double-terminated quartz crystals are found in weathered dolostone outcrops and talus slopes. The site offers accessible collecting on private land near the Town of Little Falls. Always confirm access and get permission from the landowner before collecting.

faq.question: What is a Herkimer Diamond and why are they found only here?
faq.answer: Herkimer Diamonds are clear, naturally faceted quartz crystals with distinctive pointed terminations on both ends. They formed 450 million years ago in the Ordovician Mohawk Dolomite formation when silicon-rich groundwater filled cavities in the rock. This specific geological combination and crystal habit occurs naturally in significant quantities only in Herkimer County, New York.

faq.question: Is Diamond Mountain Mining beginner-friendly for rockhounding?
faq.answer: Yes, this site is excellent for beginners. The gentle terrain at 635 feet elevation is easy to navigate, and crystals are accessible in loose rock and weathered outcrops without requiring heavy excavation. Children as young as 6 can participate. Small to medium crystals are commonly found with patience, making it rewarding for newcomers to rockhounding.

faq.question: What tools and equipment do I need to collect at this Herkimer Diamond location?
faq.answer: A small rock hammer or chisel, safety glasses, a collecting bag, and a magnifying glass are helpful. Since many crystals weather naturally from the host rock, minimal equipment is often sufficient. Sturdy hiking boots protect your feet on the rocky terrain. A field guide to Herkimer Diamonds helps identify specimens.

faq.question: What is the best time of year to rockhound for Herkimer Diamonds in New York?
faq.answer: May through October is ideal for collecting at Diamond Mountain Mining. Late spring through early fall offers comfortable temperatures and good visibility. Early fall is particularly excellent. Summer collecting is possible but can be warm. Winter freezing and thawing expose new material but makes access difficult.

faq.question: Can I bring my family and dog rockhounding at Diamond Mountain Mining?
faq.answer: Yes, this site is family-friendly and dog-friendly on private land. The gentle slope and short walking distances make it suitable for children as young as 6. Dogs are welcome. Always get permission from the landowner, respect property boundaries, and pack out all trash to maintain access for future collectors.

meta_title: Diamond Mountain Mining NY - Herkimer Diamonds
meta_description: Find Herkimer Diamonds and quartz crystals near Little Falls, NY. Easy beginner-friendly rockhounding in Herkimer County. Private land collecting.
